channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Report: Russia's nuclear-powered 'Skyfall' missile is dirty and dangerous
item.description: MIT researchers think they've worked out exactly how Russia's Burevestnik nuclear-powered missile flies. "It's almost certainly a terrible idea," one analyst said. "But it's not an impossible idea."
item.content:encoded:
MIT researchers think they've worked out exactly how Russia's Burevestnik nuclear-powered missile flies. "It's almost certainly a terrible idea," one analyst said. "But it's not an impossible idea."

(Image credit: Russian Ministry of Defense)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: Ukraine strikes Moscow refinery in one of its largest drone attacks yet
item.description: Ukraine carried out massive drone strikes on Moscow Thursday, in what appears to be its largest attack on Russia yet.
item.content:encoded: Ukraine carried out massive drone strikes on Moscow Thursday, in what appears to be its largest attack on Russia yet.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: Trump signs preliminary agreement with Iran
item.description: President Trump signed the framework agreement to end the Iran war, which includes terms that Iran won't build a nuclear weapon and could allow it to rebuild its shattered economy.
item.content:encoded: President Trump signed the framework agreement to end the Iran war, which includes terms that Iran won't build a nuclear weapon and could allow it to rebuild its shattered economy.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: How Trump's preliminary agreement with Iran compares to Obama's 2015 deal
item.description: The Trump administration released the full text of the U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding. Steve Inskeep asks former U.S. Special Envoy to Iran Robert Malley how it compares to the 2015 Iran deal.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration released the full text of the U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding. Steve Inskeep asks former U.S. Special Envoy to Iran Robert Malley how it compares to the 2015 Iran deal.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Fed holds interest rates steady and hints at future rate hike
item.description: The Federal Reserve held its benchmark interest rate steady Wednesday, and hinted its next move could be a rate increase. The new Fed chairman vowed to bring inflation under control.
item.content:encoded: The Federal Reserve held its benchmark interest rate steady Wednesday, and hinted its next move could be a rate increase. The new Fed chairman vowed to bring inflation under control.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Sen. Elissa Slotkin on intel chief's confirmation hearing and election integrity
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep talks to Democratic Sen. Elissa Slotkin of Michigan about the fight over the confirmation of a new national intelligence director, the renewal of spy tools and election integrity.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep talks to Democratic Sen. Elissa Slotkin of Michigan about the fight over the confirmation of a new national intelligence director, the renewal of spy tools and election integrity.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: The price of jet fuel is falling, but don't expect airfares to follow any time soon
item.description: The average price of jet fuel has fallen to its lowest level since the beginning of the war with Iran. But aviation experts say the cost of airfare is likely to stay high, at least for now.
item.content:encoded:
The average price of jet fuel has fallen to its lowest level since the beginning of the war with Iran. But aviation experts say the cost of airfare is likely to stay high, at least for now.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Poll: Most Americans have the summer blues about Trump and the economy
item.description: A new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds a record low share of Americans approve of President Trump's job performance and his handling of the economy heading into the summer before a key midterm election.
item.content:encoded:
A new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds a record low share of Americans approve of President Trump's job performance and his handling of the economy heading into the summer before a key midterm election.

(Image credit: Charlie Neibergall)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump signs the framework agreement to end Iran war, Trump attempts to leverage DNI confirmation to advance unrelated legislation, Trump sees record low approval on the economy in new NPR poll.
item.content:encoded: Trump signs the framework agreement to end Iran war, Trump attempts to leverage DNI confirmation to advance unrelated legislation, Trump sees record low approval on the economy in new NPR poll.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Chloe Malle, American Vogue's new head of editorial content, on the magazine's future
item.description: For NPR's Newsmakers, Michel Martin speaks about the future of American Vogue with its new Head of Editorial Content Chloe Malle.
item.content:encoded: For NPR's Newsmakers, Michel Martin speaks about the future of American Vogue with its new Head of Editorial Content Chloe Malle.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin
